frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Dekh, I'm not saying all women are like this, but you have to be practical these days. The way divorce laws are, especially alimony, it's become a huge risk for men. I know so many cases where guys get trapped, lose half their assets. And don't even get me started on 498A, it's so easily misused. Better to be cautious, protect yourself financially before taking the plunge.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, spoken by a 44-year-old Delhi director, exemplifies the 'Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers' frame by highlighting the perceived financial risks for men in marriage due to 'skewed' divorce laws and the 'misuse' of IPC 498A. It frames women who seek financial support post-divorce as potential exploiters, rather than victims, and advises men to 'protect yourself financially' – a surface manifestation of the frame.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
